name: Lithuania

authority.name: Ryšių reguliavimo tarnyba (RRT) — Communications Regulatory Authority of the Republic of Lithuania, Lithuania's eIDAS supervisory body and national Trusted List scheme operator
authority.operator: Accredited QTSPs under eIDAS (Asmens dokumentų išrašymo centras prie LR VRM / ADIC, VĮ Registrų centras / RCSC, and Migracijos departamentas prie LR VRM / MD, among others)

governingLaw: Reglamento eIDAS (UE) 910/2014 · Lietuvos Respublikos elektroninės atpažinties ir elektroninių operacijų patikimumo užtikrinimo paslaugų įstatymas

technology.trustList: ETSI TS 119 612 — the Lithuanian Trusted List (LT-TSL.xml, RRT)

relatedLinks.label: EU LOTL — List of the Lists (European Commission)

notes: Lithuania's qualified-signature trust is a federation of accredited QTSPs (Asmens dokumentų išrašymo centras prie LR VRM / ADIC, VĮ Registrų centras / RCSC, and Migracijos departamentas prie LR VRM / MD, among others) supervised by Ryšių reguliavimo tarnyba (RRT), the Communications Regulatory Authority of the Republic of Lithuania acting as Lithuania's eIDAS supervisory body, and published in the Lithuanian national eIDAS Trusted List (LT-TSL.xml, ETSI TS 119 612). As an EU / eIDAS participant, Lithuania is referenced from the EU LOTL. The 11 currently-granted CA certificates were promoted wholesale after verifying the Trusted List's XAdES signature through the EU LOTL chain of trust: the LOTL signature was checked against the pinned European Commission signing anchor, and the Lithuanian TSL's signature (signer: C=LT, O=Communications Regulatory Authority of the Republic of Lithuania, CN=Tomas Lavrinavičius; cert SHA-256 cfaa26cd8a4837d90a89216d7f7e15cdf00cbbcd9edb0711705cfe0420859978) against the identity the LOTL declares for Lithuania, so the list's single seal vouches for every certificate it contains (eIDAS Art. 22). Only services with a currently-granted status and a CA / qualified-certificate service type were selected; withdrawn, deprecated, and non-CA services (OCSP, qualified-certificate issuance profiles) were dropped. Verification evidence is in VERIFICATION.md. Key algorithms across the set are mixed: RSA-2048, RSA-4096, and EC P-384. All 11 entries are QTSP issuing CAs (ADIC CA-A / CA-B / CA ECC, RCSC IssuingCA / IssuingCA-2, and MD CA); the self-signed ADIC Root CA, ADIC Root CA ECC, RCSC RootCA, and MD Root CA that issue them are not carried by the list and must be sourced separately if root-anchored path building is required. Several older issuing-CA generations in the set have passed their notAfter and are retained as archived-but-granted entries; they surface under the Expired status filter.
